Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuiness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-16-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Roger Lee (650.219.8077; rlee82@gmail.com) Defendant in propria persona.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Not argued. Discussed re: Mr. Lee's question/seeking clarification.
The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROGER LEE's Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions:

Defendant ROGER LEE ("Defendant") moves a second time to have Requests for Admission ("Requests") purportedly propounded to Plaintiff Nathan Bareman ("Plaintiff") be deemed admitted, to establish the genuineness of documents, and for sanctions. Both parties appear pro se. After reviewing the moving papers, opposition and this court's docket, the court makes the following findings and ruling.

Defendant's initial motion was heard on March 11, 2022 and denied, without prejudice, due to lack of proof of the initial service of the Requests, among other deficiencies. The instant motion has not cured what was lacking in the initial motion. The exhibits to Defendant's instant motion do not include any proof of service of the initial Requests. Defendant seems to be inferring that the Requests were also served and propounded as an exhibit to his initial motion. Attaching discovery as an exhibit to a motion does result in them being deemed properly propounded and served. At that stage, they were merely an exhibit to a motion. Since the initial ruling was without prejudice to the refiling of the motion, it is not considered a motion for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008.

Based on the foregoing, Defendant's motion and his request for sanctions is DENIED.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sspaulding@bcsslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Spaulding, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuiness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-29-2022 Continued To May-16-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Defendant ROGER LEE (Tel: 650-219-8077) in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ROGER LEE's Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and the defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter discussed and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion and Requests for Admission are subject to numerous defects: the motion is not accompanied by a Proof of Service; the Notice of Motion seeks to establish genuineness of documents but none are identified or attached; the Declaration in support of the motion refers to an attachment but none is attached; the Requests for Admission are violative of Code of Civil Procedure Section 2033(b) and (h); and the Proof of Service accompanying the Requests for Admission is inadequately addressed, refers to an exhibit but none is attached and is internally inconsistent in other respects. The Motion and requested sanctions are therefore denied without prejudice to the propounding of Code-compliant requests for admission with proper proof of service.

Defendant to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com (cc'ing the opposing side).

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
